texto: Power, of course. The primitive fear of being controlled. It does not matter whether it is an invasion from outer space or power wielded from a subterranean command post: some alien force is about to take control on us, to dominate - and, if necessary in the process, to terminate our existence. We never stop to think - or, at best, a secondary consideration is whether such a force might be for the good, that humanity might indeed be improved by such a takeover. Volition, to which we desperately cling, is the very definition of our mature completion as social beings.

We never stop to think - or, at best, a secondary consideration is whether such a force might be for the good, that humanity might indeed be improved by such a takeover. Volition, to which we desperately cling, is the very definition of our mature completion as social beings.

texto: The arrogant elimination of the Djaouts of our world must nerve us to pursue our own combative doctrine, namely: that peaceful cohabitation on this planet demands that while the upholders of any creed are free to adopt their own existential absolutes, the right of others to do the same is thereby rendered implicit and sacrosanct. Thus the creed of inquiry, of knowledge and exchange of ideas, must be upheld as an absolute, as ancient and eternal as any other.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Wole Soyinka sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “The arrogant elimination of the Djaouts of our world must nerve us to pursue our own combative do…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, humanism, creed, útil em momentos de mudanca e termino.

texto: In one form or the other, the quest for human dignity has proved to be one of the most propulsive elements for wars, civil strife and willing sacrifice. Yet the entitlement to dignity, enshrined among the 'human rights', does not aspire to being the most self-evident, essential need for human survival, such as food, or physical health. Compared to that other candidate for the basic impulse of human existence - self-preservation - it may even be deemed self-indulgent.

texto: There is only one home to the life of a river-mussel; there is only one home to the life of a tortoise; there is only one shell to the soul of man: there is only one world to the spirit of our race. If that world leaves its course and smashes on boulders of the great void, whose world will give us shelter?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Wole Soyinka que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. texto: There is something really horrific for any human being who feels he is being consumed by other people. I'm talking about a writer's critics, who don't address what you've written, but want to probe into your existence and magnify the trivia of your life without any sense of humor, without any sense of context.
explicacao: Citação de Wole Soyinka sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “There is something really horrific for any human being who feels he is being consumed by other pe…” articula reflexao, vida, people em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como people e want.

frase_original: There is something really horrific for any human being who feels he is being consumed by other people. I'm talking about a writer's critics, who don't address what you've written, but want to probe into your existence and magnify the trivia of your life without any sense of humor, without any sense of context.

texto: the very least we can live with is an agreement that does not reduce us to slaves of imposition, but makes us partners of consent. Yes, we are compelled to make peace, we submit to force majeure, but leave us at least a piece of clothing to cover our nudity. This is the motivation behind every formula of diplomatic contrivance that is sometimes described as face-saving, and wise indeed is the victor who knows that, in order to shield his own rear from the elements, he must not denude his opponents.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Wole Soyinka sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “the very least we can live with is an agreement that does not reduce us to slaves of imposition, …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, compromise, dignity, útil em momentos de dignity e diplomacy.

texto: Books and all forms of writing are terror to those who wish to suppress the truth.
explicacao: Citação de Wole Soyinka sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Books and all forms of writing are terror to those who wish to suppress the truth.” articula reflexao, writing, wish em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como wish e terror.
